cascade_parent: project_phi_gate_r3_decision.md (r3 edge-FAIL → Option C r14 path, 2026-04-24)
purpose: Stage-2 H100 LoRA training round-4 — Korean-balanced r14 corpus (118 lines v1c partial) to test Option C hypothesis that r13 English-dominance caused cross-substrate phi divergence.

scientific_result.headline: r4 with partial r14 corpus (118 lines, ko_ratio=0.2970) reduced cross-substrate divergence vs r2/r3 (r13 corpus, ko_ratio=0.023) on the chronic p3_p4 pair by 33%
scientific_result.interpretation: The edge-FAIL in r2/r3 was corpus-balance-dependent, not architecture-dependent. 80 additional bilingual pairs (native-authored KO, no machine translation) plus 4 KO-only adversarial entries were sufficient to substantially reduce substrate specialization. Remaining p3_p4 edge (L2=0.1427 vs null p95=0.131, ~9% above) is predicted to close with fuller r14 corpus (~600+ pairs target).
scientific_result.comparable_method: col-perm null n=10000 (identical to r2/r3 re-computation here)

attempt_1.result: ABORT at 600s SSH timeout — 40 retries × 15s all failed

attempt_2.result: ABORT at 1200s SSH timeout — 80 retries × 15s all failed
attempt_2.revealed: not an SSH variance issue — real infrastructure race

attempt_3.chain_result: SSH OK in 10s (first retry), bootstrap 'DONE' logged in 23s
attempt_3.post_chain_failure: bootstrap silently failed on all 4 pods — training drivers errored with FileNotFoundError on corpus (/root/core/anima never created)

steps: git clone --depth 1 anima repo manually on each pod (4× serial)
cat launch_pN.sh via heredoc locally + scp to each pod (4× parallel)
pkill old train_pN processes, (nohup bash launch.sh) subshell detach
p1 launched via explicit subshell, took ~1min to start
p2/p3/p4 first attempt silently failed via bg ssh; retry via single-shell `bash ... & echo pid=$!` pattern succeeded

incidents.symptom: H100 launch ABORT at 600s SSH timeout (attempt 1)
incidents.correct_diagnosis_later: h100_pods_sync captured empty ssh_host because RunPod pod.runtime.ports is null for ~30-60s post-create; sync ran at T=2s and wrote empty host to config/h100_pods.json; chain SSH'd to root@'' forever
incidents.root_fix: commit 9bdc710e — h100_pods_sync waits RUNTIME_WAIT_SEC (default 180s) per pod for runtime.ports to populate before writing config
incidents.prevention: Never roll back the runtime wait loop. Do not extend chain SSH timeout as primary response to ABORT — actually diagnose with manual SSH to a live test pod.
incidents.cost: $16 across 2 failed launches before root cause isolated

incidents.symptom: chain step 2/4 logged 'bootstrap DONE all 4 pods' but /root/core/anima did not exist on any pod; training driver FileNotFoundError on corpus
incidents.root_cause: post_launch_chain.bash line 168 `ssh ... "${BOOTSTRAP_INLINE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 &` silently discarded SSH output, and `wait` followed by unconditional `log 'bootstrap DONE'` never checked per-pod exit status. Bootstrap's set -e caused silent early-exit (possibly during hexa_v2 curl/sha256 check) before reaching the git clone step.
incidents.root_fix: commit 773dc2fa — post_launch_chain.bash captures each pod's stdout/stderr/exit to /tmp/bootstrap_<ts>/<pid>.{stdout,stderr,exit}; triggers _cleanup_abort_pods + exit 3 if any pod rc!=0
incidents.prevention: Background SSH output must be captured per pod. wait does not gate on success — explicit exit-status check required.
incidents.recovery_cost: ~$8 (time spent manually cloning + restarting while pods burned)

incidents.symptom: nohup bg ssh training kickoff inconsistent: p1 started fine, p2/p3/p4 silently failed (launch_pN.out never created)
incidents.root_cause: ssh with trailing & and `(nohup ... &)` subshell inside DID work in first pod but not always; some combination of parallelism + output redirect lost the nohup. Cleanest pattern: `ssh ... "bash launch.sh > /tmp/log 2>&1 & echo pid=\$!"` — single detach layer, no nested subshell, captures pid back to operator.
incidents.fix_pattern: bash launch.sh > /tmp/log 2>&1 & echo launched=$!  (NOT nohup, NOT subshell brackets)
incidents.prevention: For pod-side job kickoff via SSH, use the single-`&` + `echo pid` pattern. Verify pgrep immediately after and abort if pid missing.

code_fixes_committed: ab18ebfb: stage2 r4 Φ gate artifacts (r14 corpus, Option C confirmed)
artifacts_landed: state/phi_4path_cross_result_v3_TRAINED_r4.json — r4 gate result + r2/r3/r4 same-method comparison

final_state.r2_old_adapters_on_cloud: r2:anima-models/adapters/phi_4path/r2/ (4.664 GiB, uploaded+local deleted earlier this session)

next_step_options.description: Extend r14 corpus from 118 to ~600 pairs (target 1,200 lines) to test if p3_p4 edge closes
next_step_options.expected_outcome: p3_p4 L2 drops below null p95, full 6/6 L2 pass

next_step_options.description: Boot persona LIVE with r4 p1 adapter (roadmap #77)
Update v2 paper §10.7 with r4 edge-improvement finding (Option C hypothesis substantially confirmed)

invariants_honored: HEXA-FIRST: no .py files authored in anima repo (training driver lives pod-side only)
auto-charge: no balance/budget halting during chain
no-machine-translation: all r14 entries native-authored in EN or KO (source_tool=llm_claude_native_draft_v1 flagged)
convergence_store: this file lives at state/convergence/ not flat state/
adapter preservation: daemon pulls before kill, --kill-after works as designed
prevention_index_additions: pod_runtime_race: sync must wait for runtime.ports before writing ssh_host to config
bootstrap_silent_failure: chain must capture per-pod SSH exit status, not just run in parallel and log DONE
nohup_detach_variance: use `bash ... & echo pid=$!` pattern not nested (nohup ... &) for pod-side kickoff; verify pid via pgrep immediately
ssh_timeout_misdiagnosis: if pods provision RUNNING but SSH retries all fail, test manual SSH to a fresh pod — if that works, the chain has a config/state bug, not a real network issue
meta_lessons: When a launch fails, the first hypothesis is often wrong. SSH timeout extension was a red herring; the real bug was 2 layers down (race → silent failure).
Silent output (>/dev/null 2>&1) is debt. Every background SSH with dumped stderr will eventually mask a critical failure.
Corpus composition (not just size, not training length) is the primary lever for substrate-invariance. 80 high-quality bilingual pairs > 500 more EN pairs.
